content: I'm trying to decide between a MacBook Pro and a Dell XPS for my PhD work. I do a lot of computational linguistics research and need something powerful but also portable for my travels.
Both are excellent choices! For computational linguistics work, what specific software do you typically run? That might help narrow down the best option.
Mostly Python for NLP tasks, TensorFlow for deep learning models, and I use LaTeX for writing papers. I'm also into mechanical keyboards and was thinking about getting a USB-C dock for my setup.

The MacBook Pro would give you excellent build quality and macOS's Unix underpinnings work well with Python development. The Dell XPS offers more customization options and typically better value. Do you travel internationally often for conferences?

Yeah, I'm based in Amsterdam but I go to conferences in Japan pretty regularly. Last year I was in Tokyo for Interspeech and loved exploring the local cuisine - especially ramen and sushi. The time difference is brutal though, 7 hours from here.

That's a significant time difference! For international travel, the MacBook's battery life and universal charging might be more convenient. Have you considered what size screen you prefer for coding sessions?

I've been using a 13-inch but thinking about upgrading to 16-inch for the extra screen real estate when running multiple terminals. I also run marathons occasionally, so I need something light enough to carry during training trips.

The weight difference between the two laptops is actually pretty significant for travel. I'm leaning toward the Dell for the better price-to-performance ratio, but I'm worried about driver issues on Linux.
The XPS actually has excellent Linux support these days, especially Ubuntu. Many developers in your field prefer it for that reason. What's your budget range for this purchase?
I'm hoping to stay under €2000 if possible. I also need to factor in a good mechanical keyboard - probably a Keychron since I've heard they work well with both macOS and Linux.

With that budget, you could get a well-specced XPS 13 or 14 with plenty of RAM for your ML workloads. The Keychron K3 or K8 would be excellent companions. Do you have a preference for tactile vs linear switches?
I prefer tactile switches, probably brown ones. I've been saving up for this setup for months now. My current laptop is 5 years old and struggling with larger datasets.

Brown switches are a great choice for typing comfort during long coding sessions. With your computational needs and travel requirements, I'd recommend the Dell XPS 14 with at least 32GB RAM. The MacBook would be closer to your upper budget limit.
That's really helpful, thanks! I think I'll go with the Dell XPS 14 then. I can use the savings to invest in a better monitor for my home office in Amsterdam.
Excellent choice! The XPS 14 will serve you well for both your research and travel needs. Let me know if you need recommendations for monitors or any other peripherals to complete your setup.
